name: ecologits-vscode
description: Shows the environmental impact of your Claude Code session in the VS Code status bar (CO₂, water, energy) via the public EcoLogits API.

commands.title: EcoLogits: Cycle display mode (last / workspace / all time)

enumDescriptions: Show only the last response's impact.
Show cumulative impact for all sessions in the current workspace.
Show cumulative impact across all projects and all time.
ecologits.mode.markdownDescription: What to accumulate. Click the status bar item to cycle through modes.

ecologits.model.markdownDescription: Claude model to estimate. `auto` tracks the model the session is actually using (reads it from the transcript). You can pin a specific model instead, e.g. `claude-sonnet-4-6`. Valid values: `claude-opus-4-8`, `claude-opus-4-7`, `claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-opus-4-5`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `claude-haiku-4-5`.

ecologits.zone.markdownDescription: Electricity-mix zone for the server location (ISO-3166 alpha-3). Defaults to `WOR` (world average). Examples: `FRA`, `USA`, `DEU`.

ecologits.metrics.markdownDescription: Space-separated list of metrics to display, in order. Available: `gwp` 🔥 (CO₂eq), `wcf` 💧 (water), `energy` ⚡ (kWh), `adpe` ⛏ (minerals), `pe` 🛢 (primary energy), `model` 🤖 (model name). Example: `model gwp wcf energy`.

ecologits.api.markdownDescription: EcoLogits estimations endpoint. Override if you self-host the API.
